markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Archer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1885) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 157

**Archetype** [Archer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=47)

</column>

---

You become trained in all simple and martial weapons in the [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3) weapon group. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in certain weapons, you also gain that proficiency rank in all simple and martial weapons in the bow weapon group. If you are at least an expert in the bow you are using, you gain access to the critical specialization effect with that bow.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the archer archetype.

<title level="2">Archer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Advanced Bow Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=1887), [Archer's Aim](/Feats.aspx?ID=1889), [Crossbow Terror](/Feats.aspx?ID=1888), [Quick Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=1886), [Staggering Fire](/Feats.aspx?ID=3616)
name: Archer Dedication

    right="Feat 6"

[Crossbow Terror](/Feats.aspx?ID=1888) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Archer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=47)

**Prerequisites**
[Archer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1885)

</column>

---

You are a dynamo with the crossbow. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to damage with crossbows. If the crossbow is a simple weapon, also increase the damage die size for your attacks made with that crossbow by one step. As normal, this damage die increase can't be combined with other abilities that alter the weapon damage die (such as the ranger feat [Crossbow Ace](/Feats.aspx?ID=491)).
name: Crossbow Terror

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

</column>

You slow down, focus, and take a careful shot. Make a ranged Strike with a weapon in the [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3) weapon group. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll and ignore the target's [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition. If the target is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), reduce the flat check from being hidden from 11 to 5.
name: Archer's Aim

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Assassin Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 158

**Archetype** [Assassin](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=48)

**Prerequisites**
[Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752); Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

</column>

---

You've trained to assassinate your foes, and you do so with tenacity and precision. You gain the Mark for Death activity.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Mark for Death;    **Requirements** You can see and hear the creature you intend to mark; **Effect** You designate a single creature as your mark. This lasts until the mark dies or you use Mark for Death again. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) your mark and on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) against your mark. Your [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) weapons and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks gain the [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172) and [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) weapon traits when you're attacking your mark. If the weapon or unarmed attack already has the deadly trait, increase the size of the deadly damage die by one step instead of giving it deadly d6.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the assassin archetype.

<title level="2">Assassin Dedication leads to...</title>
[Angel of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=1893), [Angel of Vindication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3612), [Assassinate](/Feats.aspx?ID=1894), [Eclipsed Vitality](/Feats.aspx?ID=3613), [Expert Backstabber](/Feats.aspx?ID=1891), [Public Execution](/Feats.aspx?ID=4143), [Surprise Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=1892)
name: Assassin Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752); Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Angel of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=1893) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Assassin Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890)

</column>

---

All your Strikes against a creature you have Marked for Death have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) trait, causing the mark to be instantly killed when reduced to 0 Hit Points. When killed in this way, attempts to communicate with it, return it to life, turn it into an undead, or otherwise disturb its afterlife fail unless the effect's [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) level is higher than half your level when you killed the creature (rounded up), or originates from an [artifact](/Equipment.aspx?Category=45) or a deity.
name: Angel of Death

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Assassinate](/Feats.aspx?ID=1894) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 158

**Archetype** [Assassin](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=48)

**Prerequisites**
[Assassin Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890)

**Requirements**
You have designated a mark using [Mark for Assassination](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890) and are completely [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by your mark.

</column>

---

You strike with one swift movement, trying to instantly slay your mark. Make a Strike against your mark. If you hit, your mark takes 6d6 extra precision damage with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. If the mark critically fails, they die. This is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effect. The creature then becomes temporarily immune to your Assassinate for 1 day.
name: Assassinate
requirement_markdown: You have designated a mark using [Mark for Assassination](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890) and are completely [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by your mark.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the bastion archetype.

<title level="2">Bastion Dedication leads to...</title>
[Destructive Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=1898), [Disarming Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=1896), [Drive Back](/Feats.aspx?ID=3617), [Nimble Shield Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=1897), [Shield Salvation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1899)

</title>

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Nimble Shield Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=1897) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Bastion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1895)

</column>

---

You are so used to wielding a shield that you can do so even while using the hand that's holding it for other purposes. The hand you use to wield a shield counts as a free hand for the purposes of the Interact action. You can also hold another object in this hand (but you still can't use it to wield a weapon). This benefit doesn't apply to [tower shields](/Shields.aspx?ID=4), which are still too cumbersome.
name: Nimble Shield Hand

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Destructive Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=1898) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 159

**Archetype** [Bastion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=49)

**Prerequisites**
[Bastion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1895)

</column>

---

You can protect yourself more effectively, at the expense of your shield. When you [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839), you can reduce the damage to yourself by double the shield's Hardness, but if you do, the shield takes double the normal amount of damage it would have taken (before applying its Hardness). You can't use Destructive Block if your shield can't be broken or destroyed, such as if you're using an [_indestructible shield_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=322).
name: Destructive Block

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shield Salvation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1899) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 159

**Archetypes** [Bastion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=49), [Bastion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=240) (Level 12)

**Prerequisites**
[Bastion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1895)

</column>

---

You can save your shield from total destruction, even after a devastating attack. If a shield would be destroyed due to damage taken during your [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839), the shield remains intact at 1 Hit Point instead. Its construction is weakened until you take the time to repair it, preventing you from using Shield Salvation to save the same shield until your next preparations.
name: Shield Salvation

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Beastmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1900) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 160

**Archetype** [Beastmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=50)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

You gain the service of a [young animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=151) that travels with you and obeys your commands. The rules for animal companions appear [here](Companions.aspx). Contrary to the usual rules for animal companions, this feat can grant you a second animal companion. If you ever have more than one animal companion, you gain the Call Companion action. See the Beastmaster Animal Companions sidebar for details on this action.

 Certain beastmaster feats give you primal focus spells. The rules for focus spells appear [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=276). When you gain your first beastmaster focus spell, you become trained in primal spell attack rolls and spell DCs, and your spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma. Feats that grant beastmaster focus spells tell you to increase the number of Focus Points in your pool, but if you don't already have a focus pool, you instead get a focus pool with 1 Focus Point. You can [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by grooming, feeding, playing with, or otherwise tending to an animal companion.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the beastmaster archetype.

<title level="2">Beastmaster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Additional Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=1901), [Beastmaster Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=1906), [Beastmaster's Call](/Feats.aspx?ID=1907), [Beastmaster's Trance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1904), [Bestial Protection](/Feats.aspx?ID=5424), [Billowing Wings](/Feats.aspx?ID=5433), [Ferocious Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=5428), [Heal Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=1902), [Heightened Instincts](/Feats.aspx?ID=5434), [Mature Beastmaster Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=1903), [Nature's Precision](/Feats.aspx?ID=5425), [Pack Movement](/Feats.aspx?ID=5427), [Running Kick](/Feats.aspx?ID=5429), [Sinking Jaws](/Feats.aspx?ID=5430), [Spirit of the Beast](/Feats.aspx?ID=5423), [Sweeping Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=5431), [Tenacious Endurance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5426), [Vicious Rend](/Feats.aspx?ID=5432)
name: Beastmaster Dedication

<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />

**Prerequisites**
[Beastmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1900); Call Companion

</column>

---

You quickly call in a primal projection of a non-active companion to provide the companion's support benefit. The projection arrives in an unoccupied square of your choice within 30 feet of you, grants you its support benefit, and then disappears on your next turn. The projection has the same AC and saving throw modifiers as the real companion, and if it would take any damage before your next turn, it disappears and the support benefit ends immediately.
name: Beastmaster's Call

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

**Prerequisites**
[Mature Beastmaster Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=1903); you have multiple animal companions

</column>

---

You can have up to two animal companions active at once. However, when you do, it's slightly more difficult to [Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) them. If you don't Command either of your companions, one of the two (your choice) can still use 1 action on your turn to Stride or Strike, as per [Mature Beastmaster Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=1903), but not both. When you Command an Animal, either choose one of the companions to take 2 actions, as normal, or else both companions can take 1 action to Stride or Strike. Either way, you can't Command an Animal to make either companion act again until your next turn.

<title level="2">Lead the Pack leads to...</title>
[Pack Takedown](/Feats.aspx?ID=5435)
name: Lead the Pack
prerequisite_markdown: [Mature Beastmaster Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=1903); you have multiple animal companions

You are touched by a deity and gifted with the ability to alleviate the suffering of others. You gain the [_lay on hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=395) devotion spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell. This feat grants a focus pool of 1 Focus Point that you can recover using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity. You can Refocus by meditating, whether you reflect on the deity granting the blessing or not, allowing your blessing to refill your focus pool. Your devotion spells from the blessed one archetype are divine spells.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the blessed one archetype.

<title level="2">Blessed One Dedication leads to...</title>
[Blessed Denial](/Feats.aspx?ID=1913), [Blessed Sacrifice](/Feats.aspx?ID=1911), [Blessed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1912)

When you focus your magic on an ally, you can remove harmful conditions. If your next action is to Cast a Spell from a spell slot, and that spell targets only a single ally, you can also attempt to remove a harmful condition from that ally. The condition must be one that could be removed by your [Mercy](/Feats.aspx?ID=225) feat, including those granted by later feats such as [Greater Mercy](/Feats.aspx?ID=232). Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check based on the spell's DC and level. This effect is in addition to the normal effects of your spell.
name: Blessed Spell
prerequisite_markdown: [Blessed One Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1910); ability to cast spells from spell slots; [Mercy](/Feats.aspx?ID=225)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bounty Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1914) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

**Archetype** [Bounty Hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=52)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

</column>

---

When focused on finding your quarry, you're relentless. You gain the [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10) action. You can use Hunt Prey to designate only a creature that you've seen, heard about, or learned about through some other means, such as a bounty board or wanted poster. In addition to the other benefits of Hunt Prey, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) regarding your prey.

 If you already have Hunt Prey, you become an expert in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) and gain the circumstance bonus to Gather Information about your prey; you can still designate a creature you're tracking during exploration, in addition to the conditions above.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the bounty hunter archetype.

<title level="2">Bounty Hunter Dedication leads to...</title>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tools of the Trade](/Feats.aspx?ID=1915) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 163

**Archetype** [Bounty Hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=52)

**Prerequisites**
[Bounty Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1914)

</column>

---

You're well-versed in weapons that allow you to bring bounties in alive. You are trained with the following weapons: [bola](/Weapons.aspx?ID=123), [sap](/Weapons.aspx?ID=37), and [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48). You gain access to bolas. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in a given weapon or weapons, you also gain that proficiency in the weapons listed.

 In addition, you take no penalty when making a nonlethal attack with a weapon without the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait.
name: Tools of the Trade

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Posse](/Feats.aspx?ID=1916) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Exploration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=66" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 163

**Archetype** [Bounty Hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=52)

**Prerequisites**
[Bounty Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1914)

**Requirements**
You have designated prey with Hunt Prey.

</column>

---

By spending 1 minute giving guidance to help hunt down your prey, you instruct up to five willing creatures to assist you. They gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) your prey, to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) your prey, and to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) about your prey. You and the creatures assisting you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls when entering combat with your prey.

 This benefit lasts until you designate a new prey or your prey dies, whichever comes first. An individual creature assisting you loses this benefit if they're out of your presence for too long to benefit from your instructions. This is usually 1 hour, but is determined by the GM.
name: Posse
requirement_markdown: You have designated prey with Hunt Prey.

>
[Keep Pace](/Feats.aspx?ID=1917) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Bounty Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1914)

**Trigger**
Your prey is within reach and attempts to move away from you.

</column>

---

Your prey can't escape. Stride up to your Speed, following the foe and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or you've moved your full Speed. You can use Keep Pace to [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) instead of Stride if you have the corresponding movement type.
name: Keep Pace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cavalier Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1919) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 164

**Archetype** [Cavalier](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=53)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) or Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

You gain a [young animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=151) that serves as your mount. You can choose from [animal companions](Companions.aspx) with the mount special ability, as well as any additional options from your pledge, as determined by your GM. You must choose an animal companion that's at least one size larger than you, but if the animal usually starts as Small, you can begin with a Medium version of that animal (changing no statistics other than its size).

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the cavalier archetype. This restriction is waived if you have pledged to the organization associated with the other dedication feat. For example, if you are pledged to a Hellknight order, you could take Hellknight dedication feats without needing to gain other feats from the cavalier archetype first.

<title level="2">Cavalier Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cavalier's Banner](/Feats.aspx?ID=1920), [Cavalier's Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1921), [Defend Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=1924), [Impressive Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=1922), [Legendary Rider](/Feats.aspx?ID=1930), [Mounted Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=1925), [Quick Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=1923), [Trampling Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1927), [Unseat](/Feats.aspx?ID=1928)
name: Cavalier Dedication

---

You fly the banner of your pledge from your mount, raising your allies' spirits. You and all allies within 30 feet gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves and DCs against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects. If your banner is destroyed or removed, allies within 30 feet become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).

<title level="2">Cavalier's Banner leads to...</title>
[Stalwart Standard](/Feats.aspx?ID=3620), [Wave the Flag](/Feats.aspx?ID=3621)

[Incredible Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=1926)

You've trained with your shield to defend both yourself and your mount. When you [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98) while mounted, both you and your mount gain the shield's circumstance bonus to AC. If you have the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction, you can use it in response to your mount taking damage, as long as you're riding your mount. If you do, the shield prevents your mount from taking damage instead of preventing you from taking damage, following the normal rules for Shield Block.

<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />

**Prerequisites**
[Cavalier Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1919)

**Requirements**
You are riding a mount that has a melee Strike using its legs (claw, talons, hoof, etc.).

</column>

---

You urge your mount forward, trampling enemies in your path. You command your mount to Stride up to its Speed (or to [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), if it has the corresponding movement type), moving through the spaces of any foes in your path up to one size smaller than your mount. Your mount deals damage equal to the melee Strike using its legs to each creature whose space you move through, subject to a basic Reflex save against your mount's [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) DC. On a critical failure, the creature also becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of your next turn. You can damage a given creature only once during this movement.
name: Trampling Charge

---

You attempt to knock an opponent off their mount. Make a melee Strike against a mounted opponent. If your attack hits, attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the opponent's Fortitude DC. If you succeed, the foe is knocked off its mount into a space of its choice adjacent to its mount. If you critically succeed, it lands [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Unseat

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 166 **Archetype** [Celebrity](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=54) --- The more people you have looking at you, the more content you are, and you take every opportunity to show up others when their performances are less than perfect. You gain the Upstage reaction. In addition, when you [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23), if the level of the task is higher than your level, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your check to Earn Income. **Activate** Upstage; **Trigger** A foe attempts a skill check and doesn't get a critical success; **Effect** After your foe has tried their best, you show everyone how it's really done. Attempt a check using the same skill that triggered this reaction. **Critical Success** You gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks until the end of your next turn.
**Success** As critical success, except you gain the benefits only if the triggering creature failed their skill check. **Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the celebrity archetype. Celebrity Dedication leads to... [Acknowledge Fan](/Feats.aspx?ID=4150), [Big Debut](/Feats.aspx?ID=4146), [Command Attention](/Feats.aspx?ID=1934), [Feeling Your Oats](/Feats.aspx?ID=4139), [Grudging Compliment](/Feats.aspx?ID=4141), [Mesmerizing Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=1933), [Never Tire](/Feats.aspx?ID=1932), [Statement Strut](/Feats.aspx?ID=4145)
name: Celebrity Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Never Tire](/Feats.aspx?ID=1932) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 166

**Archetype** [Celebrity](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=54)

**Prerequisites**
[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1931)

**Trigger**
You would gain the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition.

**Requirements**
You are [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) by at least three creatures who aren't foes.

</column>

---

As long as you have an audience, you can continue to perform. Indeed, you must—you have an obligation to your fans! You delay the effects of the fatigued condition for 1 minute or until you are no longer observed by the required creatures, whichever comes first. If the fatigued condition has a duration, the duration begins to elapse only after the delay. You can't further delay or prevent the fatigued condition after this ability ends.
name: Never Tire
requirement_markdown: You are [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) by at least three creatures who aren't foes.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mesmerizing Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=1933) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 166

**Archetype** [Celebrity](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=54)

**Prerequisites**
[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1931)

</column>

---

When you meet someone's gaze, they are unable to look away. Choose one target creature. That creature must succeed at a Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) until the end of your next turn; the DC for this save is equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. If the creature succeeds at its save or its fascination ends due to a hostile action, it becomes temporarily immune to your Mesmerizing Gaze for 1 day.

When you select this feat, it gains either the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11) or [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) trait; once you make this choice, you can't change it.

<title level="2">Mesmerizing Gaze leads to...</title>
[Acknowledge Fan](/Feats.aspx?ID=4150)
name: Mesmerizing Gaze

    level="1"

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Command Attention](/Feats.aspx?ID=1934) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 166

**Archetype** [Celebrity](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=54)

**Prerequisites**
[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1931)

</column>

---

You command the attention of all around you with style, ensuring their gaze falls only upon you until the end of your next turn. When creatures within 30 feet of you attempt saving throws against other [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) effects, they use the outcome for one degree of success better than the result they rolled. An enemy within the area attempting to use a visual effect that involves focusing its attention on a particular creature (such as a [medusa's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=297) Focus Gaze) must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher, in order to target any creature except you. Allies in the area can attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) even if they don't have cover, as you are continually providing a distraction.
name: Command Attention

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1935) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 167

**Archetype** [Dandy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=55)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

You are a consummate student of dignity, etiquette, and fashion. You can use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) skill to perform the special downtime activity Influence Rumor, spending at least one day of downtime to manipulate the course, tone, or content of a rumor to your benefit. The difficulty of Influencing a Rumor is determined by the GM based on the size of the community, the relative perceptiveness of the inhabitants, and the agency of other rumormongers, but it typically starts with at least DC 15 for a small village and increases to at least DC 20 for a town, at least DC 30 for a city, and at least DC 40 for a metropolis.

 You become trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); if you were already trained, you become an expert instead.

**Special** You can't select another Dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the dandy archetype.

<title level="2">Dandy Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cutting Flattery](/Feats.aspx?ID=4138), [Distracting Flattery](/Feats.aspx?ID=1936), [Fabricated Connections](/Feats.aspx?ID=1938), [Feeling Your Oats](/Feats.aspx?ID=4139), [Gossip Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=1937), [Party Crasher](/Feats.aspx?ID=1939), [Statement Strut](/Feats.aspx?ID=4145), [Tut-Tut](/Feats.aspx?ID=4136)
name: Dandy Dedication

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 167 **Archetypes** [Dandy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=55), [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311) (Level 4) **Prerequisites** [Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1935); Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) **Trigger** You observe a target's attitude toward yourself or your allies decrease as a result of an ally's behavior. --- You know how to maintain a good impression and manage your image, even while keeping uncouth company. Make a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the target's Will DC. Regardless of your result, the target is temporarily immune to your Distracting Flattery for 10 minutes. **Success** The target's attitude doesn't decrease as a result of your ally's social blunder.
**Failure** The target's attitude decreases, as normal.
**Critical Failure** Your attempt makes matters worse, decreasing the target's attitude toward you by one step, in addition to any changes from the behavior that triggered this reaction.
name: Distracting Flattery

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1935)

</column>

---

Your time gossiping in fashionable salons keeps you informed on every topic. You are trained in [Gossip Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), a special Lore skill that can be used only to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), but on any topic. If you fail a check to Recall Knowledge with Gossip Lore, you get the effects of the [Dubious Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=776) skill feat.

 If you have legendary proficiency in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14), you gain expert proficiency in Gossip Lore, but you can't increase your proficiency rank in Gossip Lore by any other means.
name: Gossip Lore

You can lie about having taken an action so convincingly that you acquire all the tangible benefits of having successfully completed it. You can roll [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) in place of a different skill when attempting to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23), [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51), or [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=27). You can use Fabricated Connections to Make an Impression or Request once per day, and you can also use it once per week to Earn Income or Subsist.

>
[Party Crasher](/Feats.aspx?ID=1939) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Prerequisites**
[Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1935); Master in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

You attend all the fashionable society parties, no matter how exclusive they might be. Any time you encounter a social event you would ordinarily be denied access to, such as a coronation, royal gala, or other society function, you can spend 1d4 hours to secure entry without the need for a skill check. You find invitations, invitees looking for fashionable dates, temporary jobs with the caterers, event staff willing to look the other way, or some other mode of access for yourself and your allies. This ability doesn't apply to secret events or other small private gatherings with no staff, dates, or outsiders involved.
name: Party Crasher

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 168 **Archetype** [Dragon Disciple](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=56) **Access** You are a kobold with the dragonscaled or spellscaled heritage, a dragon instinct barbarian, or a draconic bloodline sorcerer. --- You choose to study or worship one type of dragon, and your focus grants you a measure of its power. You become trained in arcane spell DCs and arcane spell attack rolls. Choose one color of dragon when you select this feat; if you are a sorcerer with the draconic bloodline, this must be the same type as your bloodline. You gain resistance equal to half your level against one type of damage determined by the chosen dragon type. The GM may allow you to choose a dragon type not listed below, and will determine the damage type appropriate for that dragon.
  • **[Black](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=165), [Brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=175)*, or [Copper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=172)** Acid
  • **[Blue](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=166), [Bronze](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=171), [Cloud](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176)*, or [Sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=229)*** Electricity
  • **[Brass](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=170), [Gold](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=173), [Magma](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=178)*, [Red](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168), or [Underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=231)*** Fire
  • **[Crystal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=177)* or [Forest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=227)*** Piercing
  • **[Green](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=167)** Poison
  • **[Sea](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=228)*** Bludgeoning
  • **[Silver](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=174) or [White](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=169)** Cold
  • **[Sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=230)*** Mental
  • **[Umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=179)*** Negative
    ***Source** [_Mwangi Expanse pg. 76_](https://paizo.com/products/btq027ot)
You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) effects and effects that would make you [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). **Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the dragon disciple archetype. If you later take the [sorcerer archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=11), you must choose the draconic bloodline. Dragon Disciple Dedication leads to... [Breath of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1945), [Claws of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1941), [Cloud Walk](/Feats.aspx?ID=3406), [Deflecting Cloud](/Feats.aspx?ID=3408), [Disciple's Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=1948), [Draconic Scent](/Feats.aspx?ID=1942), [Dragon Arcana](/Feats.aspx?ID=1943), [Mighty Wings](/Feats.aspx?ID=3409), [Scales of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1944), [Shape of the Cloud Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=3407), [Shape of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1947), [Wings of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1946)
name: Dragon Disciple Dedication

---

Your fingernails grow into razor-sharp claws, and scales speckle your fingers, hands, and wrists. Your claws are [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks that deal 1d6 slashing damage and are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group.

 If you're a draconic sorcerer, when you cast [_dragon claws_](/Spells.aspx?ID=500), increase the spell's slashing damage die from d4 to d6 and increase the resistance to 10 at 1st level, 15 at 5th level, and 20 at 9th level.
name: Claws of the Dragon

Add the draconic bloodline's granted spells to your spell list; you must still learn them or add them to your repertoire as normal. These spells are [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [_true strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=345), [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256), [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [_spell immunity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=296), [_chromatic wall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=37), [_dragon form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=88), [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=185), [_prismatic wall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=234), and [_overwhelming presence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=212).

Scales grow across your body, protecting you against physical and magical threats. When you're unarmored, the scales give you a +2 item bonus to AC with a Dexterity cap of +3. The item bonus to AC from Scales of the Dragon is cumulative with armor potency runes on your explorer's clothing, mage armor, and bracers of armor. Your resistance from Dragon Disciple Dedication increases to 3 + half your level.

You've discovered how to transform yourself into a dragon. Once per day, you can cast 7th-level [_dragon form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=88) as an innate arcane spell, transforming into your chosen type of dragon. The spell automatically heightens to 8th level if you're 16th level and 9th level if you're 18th level. Any time you score a critical hit with an unarmed Strike gained from _dragon form_, you recharge the spell's breath weapon immediately.

[Mighty Dragon Shape](/Feats.aspx?ID=1949)

Any effects from these feats that apply to one-handed melee weapons or melee Strikes also apply to one-handed ranged weapons and ranged Strikes.

[Dual-Weapon Warrior Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1950)

You are wielding two one-handed weapons, each in a different hand, one of which is a ranged weapon.

</column>

---

You carry your ammunition in a way that allows you to reload while holding two weapons. You Interact to reload a one-handed ranged weapon you're holding. Unlike most Interact actions, you don't need a free hand to reload your ranged weapon in this way.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Flensing Slice](/Feats.aspx?ID=1953) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Dual-Weapon Warrior Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1950)

**Requirements**
Your last action was a [Double Slice](/Feats.aspx?ID=356), and both attacks hit the target.

</column>

---

When you hit with both attacks with Double Slice, you flense the target, making it bleed and creating a weak spot. The target takes 1d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) per weapon damage die of whichever of the weapons you used that has the most weapon damage dice (maximum 4d8 for a [_major striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) weapon). The target becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), and its resistances to any physical damage types are reduced by 5; these two effects last until the beginning of your next turn.
name: Flensing Slice
requirement_markdown: Your last action was a [Double Slice](/Feats.aspx?ID=356), and both attacks hit the target.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Duelist's Challenge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1957) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Open" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=123" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 171

**Archetype** [Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=58)

**Prerequisites**
[Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1956)

</column>

---

Select one foe that you can see and proclaim a challenge. That foe is your dueling opponent until they are defeated, flee, or the encounter ends. Any time you hit that enemy using a single one-handed melee weapon while your other hand or hands are free, you gain a circumstance bonus to the Strike's damage equal to the number of damage dice your weapon deals.

 If you attack a creature other than your dueling opponent, you take a circumstance penalty to damage equal to the number of damage dice your weapon deals.
name: Duelist's Challenge
search_markdown:
<traits>

You protect those near you with a flash of steel. When you're benefiting from [Dueling Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=367), allies adjacent to you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC. If you have [Dueling Riposte](/Feats.aspx?ID=389), you can use it when an enemy within your reach critically fails a Strike against an ally adjacent to you, not just against yourself.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Student of the Dueling Arts](/Feats.aspx?ID=1959) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=58)

**Prerequisites**
[Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1956)

</column>

---

You have studied a great many combat techniques, which you can review each day. During your daily preparations, you can swap out any number of your duelist archetype feats for other duelist archetype feats of the appropriate level for which you are qualified. You can't swap out [Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1956) or Student of the Dueling Arts in this way.

 In addition, you can enter a stance from a duelist archetype feat you don't have (such as one listed under Additional Feats) by increasing the number of actions it takes to enter the stance by 1 (typically to 2 actions). You must still meet the feat's prerequisites.
name: Student of the Dueling Arts

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eldritch Archer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1960) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 172

**Archetype** [Eldritch Archer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=59)

**Prerequisites**
expert in at least one type of [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

You blend magic with your archery, leading to powerful results.

 If you don't already cast spells from spell slots, you learn to cast spontaneous spells and gain the Cast a Spell activity. You gain a spell repertoire with one cantrip of your choice, from a spell list of your choice. You choose this cantrip from the common spells on your chosen spell list or from other spells to which you have access on that list. This cantrip must require a spell attack roll. You're trained in spell attack rolls and spell DCs for that tradition. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

 You also gain Eldritch Shot.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Eldritch Shot;    **Requirements** You are wielding a [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3); **Effect** You Cast a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires a spell attack roll. The effects of the spell do not occur immediately but are imbued into the bow you're wielding. Make a Strike with that bow. Your spell flies with the ammunition, using your attack roll result to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for your multiple attack penalty, but you don't apply the penalty until after you've completed both attacks.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the eldritch archer archetype.

<title level="2">Eldritch Archer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Arrow of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=1968), [Basic Eldritch Archer Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=1961), [Enchanting Arrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1962), [Magic Arrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1963), [Phase Arrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1967), [Precious Arrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1964), [Seeker Arrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1966)
name: Eldritch Archer Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: expert in at least one type of [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)

With a single whisper carried on the wind, you enchant your ammunition to make a foe more vulnerable to your attacks. Make a bow Strike. On a hit, the target takes an additional 2d6 mental damage. On a critical hit, the target also becomes [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). The mental damage increases to 3d6 if your bow has a [_greater striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) rune, or to 4d6 if your bow has a _major striking_ rune.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magic Arrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1963) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 173

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Magic Arrow feat allows that character to choose any magical ammunition they have access to, not just the common ammunition from this book and the Core Rulebook._

**Archetype** [Eldritch Archer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=59)

**Frequency**
once per round

**Prerequisites**
[Eldritch Archer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1960)

</column>

---

You imbue your ammunition with eldritch power. When you select this feat, choose three types of common [magical ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=16) of 4th level or lower from the _Core Rulebook_ or this book. Your GM might allow you to choose from other types of magical ammunition, such as uncommon ammunition, or ammunition from other books.

 When using Magic Arrow, you transform a non-magical arrow or bolt into a piece of ammunition of one type you chose. You must shoot the ammunition before the end of your turn or the magic dissipates. If the ammunition has an Activate entry, you still need to spend the required actions to activate the ammunition before shooting it. When you use Magic Arrow, you can choose a type of magical ammunition that is typically not available to the type of ammunition you're using—for example, you can use [_climbing bolt_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=160) on an arrow, even though that magical ammunition is normally only found on bolts.

**Special** You can select this feat multiple times. Each time you do, select three additional types of ammunition as described above.
name: Magic Arrow

You can concentrate an immense amount of magic to create a piece of ammunition that phases through everything but your target. Make a bow Strike against a foe who is [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to you (but not [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39)). The ammunition travels to your target in a straight line, passing through any non-magical barriers or walls in its way, though magical barriers stop the arrow. The shot ignores all cover, the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition, the [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) condition, and circumstance bonuses to AC from shields. It has a +4 status bonus to hit creatures wearing any type of armor. The Strike's damage can't be reduced with a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction using a non-magical shield.
name: Phase Arrow

You have forged a mystical bond with a creature. This might have involved complex rituals and invocations, such as meditating under the moon until something crept out of the forest. Or maybe you just did each other a good turn, such as rescuing the beast from a trap or a foe, and then being rescued in turn. Whatever the details, you are now comrades until the end. You gain a [familiar](Familiars.aspx). If you already have a familiar, you gain the [Enhanced Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=318) feat.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the familiar master archetype.

**Requirements**

</column>

---

Under your tutelage, your familiar has grown attuned to the hidden currents of the world and can serve as a conduit for your magic. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that has a range, the spell uses the familiar as its origin point.

prerequisite_markdown: [Familiar Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1970); able to cast spells

    level="1"

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mutable Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=1974) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1970)

</column>

---

Your familiar's supernatural spirit has outgrown its corporeal body. You can conduct a special 10-minute activity to reselect certain familiar abilities, switching one or more of the following abilities for other abilities on this list: [amphibious](/Familiars.aspx?ID=1), [burrower](/Familiars.aspx?ID=2), [climber](/Familiars.aspx?ID=3), [darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=5), [fast movement](/Familiars.aspx?ID=6), [manual dexterity](/Familiars.aspx?ID=10), [resistance](/Familiars.aspx?ID=32), and [scent](/Familiars.aspx?ID=11). You can reselect only familiar abilities you would normally be able to reselect each day, not required familiar abilities for your familiar. You can't remove an ability that is required for another ability your familiar has (for instance, you can't remove manual dexterity if the familiar has [lab assistant](/Familiars.aspx?ID=9)).
name: Mutable Familiar

You know how to turn combat into a form of entertainment. You become trained in [Gladiatorial Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8); if already trained in Gladiatorial Lore, you instead become trained in another Lore skill of your choice. 

At the start of a combat encounter, if you have spectators, you gain a number of temporary HP equal to your character level for 1 minute and you can roll [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) for your initiative.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the gladiator archetype.

<title level="2">Gladiator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Big Debut](/Feats.aspx?ID=4146), [Fancy Moves](/Feats.aspx?ID=1977), [Living for the Applause](/Feats.aspx?ID=4142), [Performative Weapons Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=1980), [Play to the Crowd](/Feats.aspx?ID=1978), [Stage Fighting](/Feats.aspx?ID=1979)

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 175 **Archetype** [Gladiator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=61) **Prerequisites** [Gladiator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1976) **Trigger** You reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points during a non-trivial combat encounter with spectators. --- You show off for the crowd. Attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check; the DC is determined by the GM but is typically the standard DC for your level or the DC to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on the spectators, whichever is higher. On a success, choose one of the following benefits; on a critical success, choose two benefits:
  • A number of temporary Hit Points equal to your character level; these last for 1 minute.
  • A +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the end of your next turn.
  • A +1 circumstance bonus to your next attack roll before the end of your next turn.
Play to the Crowd leads to... [Call Your Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=1981)
name: Play to the Crowd

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 176 **Archetype** [Herbalist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=62) **Prerequisites** Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10); [Natural Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=815) --- You can create remedies and other herbal products. You gain the [basic alchemy benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1435), though they apply only for herbal items: alchemical items with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) trait, plus [antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80) and [antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=81). You gain batches of infused reagents per day equal to your level or half your level if you didn't make your daily preparations in the wilderness. Your advanced alchemy level for creating these is 1 and doesn't increase on its own. You become an expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) and can use Nature instead of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) herbal items. You don't need to be trained in Crafting or have the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752) feat to do so, and you can use [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) instead of [alchemist's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2). **Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the herbalist archetype. Herbalist Dedication leads to... [Endemic Herbs](/Feats.aspx?ID=1985), [Expert Herbalism](/Feats.aspx?ID=1986), [Fresh Ingredients](/Feats.aspx?ID=1983), [Gravelands Herbalist](/Feats.aspx?ID=3614), [Poultice Preparation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1984) Alchemical Plant Items **Source** [_Lost Omens Travel Guide pg. 12_](https://paizo.com/products/btq02dv8)
[Alchemical plant items](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=86) are “herbal items,” meaning characters with the herbalist archetype can use their archetype abilities with them.
name: Herbalist Dedication

    level="1"

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

[Herbalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1982)

</column>

---

When you create an herbal elixir, you can prepare it as a poultice instead. A poultice gains the [oil](/Traits.aspx?ID=121) trait (but remains alchemical, not magical) and loses the [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=59) and [ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=95) traits. You can apply a poultice by Interacting with one hand. When you apply a poultice, in addition to the item's normal effects, the recipient can attempt an immediate flat check to recover from a single source of [persistent acid, bleed, or fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), with the DC reduction from appropriate assistance.
name: Poultice Preparation

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 176 **Archetype** [Herbalist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=62) **Prerequisites** [Herbalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1982); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) --- During your daily preparations, you find medicinal plants endemic to your location. Until your next preparations, you can spend an additional batch of herbs to add a benefit to an item you create with your herbalism. The benefit of the special herbs affects the creature that the item is applied to; if the benefit is not immediate, it lasts for 1 minute unless otherwise stated. This adjustment counts as an [additive](/Traits.aspx?ID=4), so you can't include another additive without spoiling the item.
  • **Aquatic** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves.
  • **Arctic** For 1 hour, treat [environmental cold effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less severe.
  • **Desert** For 1 hour, treat environmental heat effects as if they were one step less severe.
  • **Forest** Gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effects.
  • **Mountain** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves.
  • **Plains** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves.
  • **Swamp** Remove one source of [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
  • **Underground** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception.
name: Endemic Herbs

You've mastered travel within a specific type of terrain. You gain the [Favored Terrain](/Feats.aspx?ID=495) feat. When in your favored terrain, you gain a +10-foot circumstance bonus to your travel Speed. When other creatures [Follow the Expert](/Actions.aspx?ID=514) with you as a guide in your favored terrain, they gain both the ability to ignore non-magical difficult terrain for the purpose of their travel Speed and the bonus to travel Speed.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the horizon walker archetype.

<title level="2">Horizon Walker Dedication leads to...</title>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Acclimatization](/Feats.aspx?ID=1988) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 177

**Archetype** [Horizon Walker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=63)

**Prerequisites**
[Horizon Walker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1987)

</column>

---

Your time spent in the hostile parts of the world has inured you to extreme climates. In your favored terrain, you gain resistance equal to half your level to all [environmental damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) and are affected by temperature effects as though they were one step less severe (incredible heat or cold becomes extreme, extreme heat or cold becomes severe, and so on). When other creatures [Follow the Expert](/Actions.aspx?ID=514) with you as a guide in your favored terrain, they too treat temperature effects as one step less severe.
name: Acclimatization

You can pronounce and repeat words with near-perfect accuracy even if you don't understand their meaning, and you can write them down in a phonetic script that allows you or another creature with Phonetic Training to read it later. </column>

---

You break down a specific individual's idiolect, memorizing their speech mannerisms and habits. If you interact with someone for at least 10 minutes, when you later attempt to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) that individual, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to your Deception checks and DCs. Due to the intense character study required, you can't remember more than one idiolect at a time.
name: Analyze Idiolect

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Read Shibboleths](/Feats.aspx?ID=1998) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 178

**Archetypes** [Linguist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=64), [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311) (Level 7)

**Prerequisites**
[Linguist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1993); Master in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

All communication is filled with slight tells and signals. If you interact with someone for at least 10 minutes, their regional words, pronunciation changes, and nonverbal cues provide you with a minor fact about their social environment, such as their hometown or certain groups they might belong to. If a target of this ability is being deceptive about their social environment, such as a commoner pretending to be a noble, they must attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14), or appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check against your Society DC, providing you a false minor fact consistent with their assumed identity on a success.

 Once you've gleaned one or more true minor facts about a person, you can then incorporate these mannerisms into your speech to present yourself in a way that they find familiar. This grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to your [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and Deception checks with them.
name: Read Shibboleths

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crude Communication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1999) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 178

**Archetypes** [Linguist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=64), [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311) (Level 8)

**Prerequisites**
[Linguist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1993)

</column>

---

Even if you don't speak a creature's language, you can rely on inflection, root words, and body language to infer rudimentary meaning. If you interact with a creature for at least 10 minutes and that creature can speak a language, the GM rolls a secret [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) check with a DC appropriate for the language's rarity. On a success, you understand the gist of the meaning and can communicate basic concepts back to the creature; on a failure, you are mistaken or communicate incorrect concepts. If you're legendary in Society, you can communicate instantly without needing to attempt a Society check; even if you didn't know the medium of communication the creature uses (speech, sign language, and so on), you intuit this information as soon as they try to communicate.
name: Crude Communication

You've compiled a vast repository of information that touches on nearly every subject. You are trained in [Loremaster Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), a special Lore skill that can be used only to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), but on any topic. If you have legendary proficiency in a skill used to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22), you gain expert proficiency in Loremaster Lore, but you can't increase your proficiency rank in Loremaster Lore by any other means. You can take feats in the loremaster's additional feats entry even if you don't meet the enigma muse prerequisite. 

If you have the [Bardic Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=179) class feat, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks with Bardic Lore.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the loremaster archetype.

<title level="2">Loremaster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Magical Edification](/Feats.aspx?ID=2001), [Orthographic Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=2002), [Quick Study](/Feats.aspx?ID=2003)

prerequisite_markdown: trained in at least one skill to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22)

[Quick Study](/Feats.aspx?ID=2003) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 179

**Archetype** [Loremaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=65)

**Prerequisites**
[Loremaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2000)

</column>

---

During your daily preparations, you can study a particular subject to gain the trained proficiency rank in one [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill of your choice. You must have interacted with that subject in some way on the previous day, whether by reading about it, talking to someone knowledgeable on the topic, or experiencing it yourself. Alternatively, you can reference your notes to instead choose a Lore skill you've prepared before with Quick Study. This proficiency lasts until you prepare again. Since this proficiency is temporary, you can't use it as a prerequisite for a skill increase or a permanent character option like a feat.
name: Quick Study

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2005) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 180

**Archetype** [Marshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=66)

**Prerequisites**
trained in martial weapons; Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

---

You've combined your social skills and combat training to become a talented combat leader. Choose [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7). You become trained in that skill or become an expert if you were already trained in it.

 In addition, you're surrounded by a marshal's aura in a 10-foot emanation. Your aura has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) traits and grants you and allies within the aura a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68).

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the marshal archetype.

<title level="2">Marshal Dedication leads to...</title>
[Back to Back](/Feats.aspx?ID=2012), [Cadence Call](/Feats.aspx?ID=2010), [Coordinated Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2015), [Devrin's Cunning Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=4134), [Dread Marshal Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2006), [Inspiring Marshal Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2007), [Push Back the Dead!](/Feats.aspx?ID=3615), [Rallying Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2011), [Snap Out of It! (Marshal)](/Feats.aspx?ID=2008), [Steel Yourself!](/Feats.aspx?ID=2009), [Target of Opportunity](/Feats.aspx?ID=2017), [To Battle!](/Feats.aspx?ID=2013), [Topple Foe](/Feats.aspx?ID=2014)
name: Marshal Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: trained in martial weapons; Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 180 **Archetype** [Marshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=66) **Prerequisites** [Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2005); Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) --- Putting on a grim face for the battle ahead, you encourage your allies to strike fear into their foes with vicious attacks. When you use this action, attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check. The DC is usually a standard-difficulty DC of your level, but the GM can assign a different DC based on the circumstances. The effect depends on the result of your check. **Critical Success** Your marshal's aura increases to a 20-foot emanation, and it grants you and allies a status bonus to damage rolls equal to the number of weapon damage dice of the unarmed attack or weapon you are wielding that has the most weapon damage dice. When you or an ally in the aura critically hits an enemy with a Strike, that enemy is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Success** As critical success, but your aura's size doesn't increase.
**Failure** You fail to enter the stance.
**Critical Failure** You fail to enter the stance and can't take this action again for 1 minute.
name: Dread Marshal Stance

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Snap Out of It! (Marshal)](/Feats.aspx?ID=2008) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 180

**Archetype** [Marshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=66)

**Prerequisites**
[Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2005)

</column>

---

You give a quick shout, hoping to shake the fog clouding your ally's thoughts. Choose one target ally within your marshal's aura who is affected by a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect that allowed a Will save and has a duration of no longer than 1 minute. That ally can immediately attempt a Will save with a +1 circumstance bonus against the effect's DC, ending the effect on a success. This can't end the effect for any creatures other than your target ally. Regardless of the result of the save, your ally is temporarily immune to Snap Out of It! for 10 minutes.
name: Snap Out of It! (Marshal)

</title>

<traits>

once per minute

**Prerequisites**
[Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2005)

</column>

---

You call out a quick cadence, guiding your allies into a more efficient rhythm. Each willing ally within your marshal's aura is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) until the end of their next turn, and they can use the extra action only to Stride. At the end of each ally's turn, if they used the extra action, they then become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the end of their following turn.

<title level="2">Cadence Call leads to...</title>
[Tactical Cadence](/Feats.aspx?ID=2016)
name: Cadence Call

Your fearless charge into battle reinvigorates your allies to carry on the fight. You Stride up to your Speed and make a melee Strike. If your Strike hits and damages an enemy, each ally within 60 feet who saw you hit gains temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier. These temporary Hit Points last until the start of your next turn.

[Back to Back](/Feats.aspx?ID=2012) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Marshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=66)

**Prerequisites**
[Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2005)

</column>

---

You excel at watching your allies' backs and helping them watch yours. As long as you and an ally are adjacent to each other, neither of you can become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) due to flanking unless both of you are flanked. If you're adjacent to more than one ally, all eligible allies can benefit at a given time. The benefit is negated for everyone if at least you and any one eligible ally are flanked, but not if your allies are flanked and you aren't.
name: Back to Back

</column>

---

You capitalize on your ally's attack to create a coordinated barrage. Make a ranged Strike with a –2 penalty against the opponent targeted by the triggering attack. This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike. If this Strike is successful, combine the damage from the attack with the damage from your ally's attack for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.
name: Target of Opportunity
requirement_markdown: You have a ranged or [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon in hand.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"

[Martial Artist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2018) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 182

**Archetype** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67)

</column>

---

You have trained to use your fists as deadly weapons. The damage die for your fist unarmed attacks becomes 1d6 instead of 1d4. You don't take the –2 circumstance penalty for making a lethal attack with your [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) unarmed attacks. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in certain weapons, you also gain that proficiency rank in all unarmed attacks.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the martial artist archetype.

<title level="2">Martial Artist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Follow-Up Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2019), [Grievous Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=2020), [Path of Iron](/Feats.aspx?ID=2021), [Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575)

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grievous Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=2020) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />

---

You know how to deliver focused, powerful blows that bypass your enemies' resistances. Make an unarmed melee Strike. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, you deal two extra weapon damage dice. If you are at least 18th level, increase this to three extra weapon damage dice.

 This attack also ignores an amount of resistance to physical damage, or to a specific physical damage type, equal to your level.

</title>

</column>

---

With a burst of effort, you weave a path through your enemies, striking each in turn as you move past them. You Stride; this movement doesn't trigger reactions. You can Strike up to three times at any point during your movement, each against a different enemy. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but your multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until you have made all your attacks.

---

You become an expert in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9). When you succeed with [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760) or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57), the target regains 5 additional HP at DC 20, 10 HP at DC 30, or 15 HP at DC 40. Once per day, you can use Battle Medicine on a creature that's temporarily immune. If you're a master in Medicine, you can do so once per hour.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you gain two other feats from the medic archetype.

<title level="2">Medic Dedication leads to...</title>
[Doctor's Visitation](/Feats.aspx?ID=2028), [Preventative Treatment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3618), [Resuscitate](/Feats.aspx?ID=2031), [Treat Condition](/Feats.aspx?ID=2029)

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 184 **Archetype** [Medic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=69) **Prerequisites** [Medic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2027) **Requirements** You are holding [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25), or you are wearing them and have a hand free. --- You treat an adjacent creature in an attempt to reduce the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition. If a creature has multiple conditions from this list, choose one. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the condition, using your [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) modifier as your counteract modifier and the condition's source to determine the DC. You can't treat a condition that came from an artifact or effect above 20th level unless you have [Legendary Medic](/Feats.aspx?ID=803); even if you do, the counteract DC increases by 10. Treating a Condition that is continually applied under certain circumstances (for instance, the enfeebled condition a good character gains from carrying an [_unholy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=307) weapon) has no effect as long as the circumstances continue. **Critical Success** Reduce the condition value by 2.
**Success** Reduce the condition value by 1.
**Critical Failure** Increase the condition value by 1. Treat Condition leads to... [Holistic Care](/Feats.aspx?ID=2030), [Preventative Treatment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3618)
name: Treat Condition
requirement_markdown: You are holding [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25), or you are wearing them and have a hand free.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />

**Prerequisites**
[Medic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2027); Legendary in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

**Requirements**
You are holding [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) or are wearing them and have a hand free. Also, the target's body is mostly intact.

</column>

---

You can use [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) to resuscitate the dead. Attempt a DC 40 Medicine check to revive a dead creature that has been dead for no more than 3 rounds. If you succeed, the target returns to life with the effects of [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243), except it still has the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition it had before [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11), increased by 1 (or wounded 1 if it wasn't wounded before dying). Whether you succeed or fail, the target is temporarily immune to Resuscitate for 1 day.
name: Resuscitate
requirement_markdown: You are holding [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) or are wearing them and have a hand free. Also, the target's body is mostly intact.

</summary>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pirate Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2032) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 185

**Archetype** [Pirate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=70)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

---

As a pirate, you sail the seas in search of enemy ships to plunder. You become trained in [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), or become an expert in it if you were already trained. You ignore the effects of difficult terrain or uneven ground caused by a ship's movement. Additionally, you gain the Boarding Assault action.

**Boarding Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) Either Stride twice or attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check (DC determined by the GM, but usually DC 20) to swing on a rope up to twice your Speed. If you boarded or disembarked from a boat or similar vehicle during this movement, you can make a melee Strike that deals one additional weapon damage die.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the pirate archetype.

<title level="2">Pirate Dedication leads to...</title>
[Broadside Buckos](/Feats.aspx?ID=4133), [Cutting Flattery](/Feats.aspx?ID=4138), [Hook 'Em](/Feats.aspx?ID=4147), [Pirate Weapon Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2033), [Rope Runner](/Feats.aspx?ID=2034), [Walk the Plank](/Feats.aspx?ID=2035), [Watch This!](/Feats.aspx?ID=4140)
name: Pirate Dedication

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

</column>

---

You run and climb across ropes and rigging almost as easily as on the ground. You gain a climb Speed of 15 feet, but only on ship's rigging or similar ropes. Whenever you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) a rope or an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) on a rope, you get a critical success instead. You aren't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) while Climbing or Balancing on a rope.
name: Rope Runner
