<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Walk the Plank](/Feats.aspx?ID=2035) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 185

**Archetype** [Pirate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=70)

**Prerequisites**
[Pirate Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2032)

</column>

---

You frighten a foe into moving where you want them, traditionally demanding they walk the plank. Attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) an opponent; this check gains the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait. On a success, in addition to the normal effects, you can also force the target to Stride up to its Speed. You choose the path the target takes, and it does so as part of your Walk the Plank action. You can't force the target to move into a harmful space (one where it will take damage, fall, provoke reactions, or similar) unless the result of your check to Demoralize was a critical success. The target then becomes temporarily immune to Walk the Plank for 24 hours.
name: Walk the Plank

</column>

---

You make a point of always having a few toxins near at hand—you never know when some arsenic or the like might prove useful. You gain the [basic alchemy benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1435), though they can be used only for [alchemical poisons](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9). You gain batches of infused reagents per day equal to your level. Your advanced alchemy level for creating these is 1 and doesn't increase on its own.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained at least two other feats from the poisoner archetype.

<title level="2">Poisoner Dedication leads to...</title>
[Acquired Tolerance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2040), [Expert Poisoner](/Feats.aspx?ID=2038), [Poison Coat](/Feats.aspx?ID=2039), [Poisoner's Twist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2037)

</traits>

[Poisoner Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2036); Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

**Requirements**
Your last action was a successful melee Strike that dealt damage against a target that is afflicted by a poison, and you know which poison.

</column>

---

Poisons attack specific parts of the body—one venom might target the lungs, another the circulatory system, while another deteriorates the nerves. You know how to take advantage of such weaknesses. You deal 1d6 damage of the required Strike's damage type and 1d6 poison damage to the target. If you're at least 18th level, you deal 2d6 damage of each type.
name: Poisoner's Twist
requirement_markdown: Your last action was a successful melee Strike that dealt damage against a target that is afflicted by a poison, and you know which poison.

>
[Poison Coat](/Feats.aspx?ID=2039) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Poisoner Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2036)

**Trigger**
A creature adjacent to you hits you with a melee unarmed Strike.

**Requirements**
You have prepared your clothing to poison attackers (see below).

</column>

---

In nature, certain animals are covered in venomous spines, such that even touching them is deadly. To use this reaction, you must have brushed poison onto your clothing or woven sharp poisoned needles into the garment's fabric. This takes 10 minutes and uses one dose of [contact](/Traits.aspx?ID=37) or [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) poison. You can have only one such poison suffused into your clothing at a time.

 When you use this reaction, the triggering creature is exposed to the suffused poison.
name: Poison Coat
requirement_markdown: You have prepared your clothing to poison attackers (see below).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Acquired Tolerance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2040) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 186

**Archetype** [Poisoner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=71)

**Prerequisites**
[Poisoner Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2036)

**Trigger**
You fail a save against a poison.

</column>

---

A small amount of poison, taken at nonlethal doses, can help the body build up a resistance against a more deadly dosage. Reroll the triggering check and use the second result. Once you use Acquired Tolerance, you can continue to use it against the same type of poison that day, but you can't use it against a different type of poison until after you make your next daily preparations. For instance, if you used the reaction on a save against [giant scorpion venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=114), you could use it again against giant scorpion venom even if it came from a different source, but you couldn't use it against [nettleweed residue](/Equipment.aspx?ID=124), [lich dust](/Equipment.aspx?ID=121), or another poison.
name: Acquired Tolerance

>
[Ritualist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2041) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 187

**Archetype** [Ritualist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=72)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You have begun to master the difficult art of casting [rituals](/Rules.aspx?ID=777). You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to all primary checks to perform a ritual. You learn two [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) [rituals](Rituals.aspx) with a level no higher than half your level; you must meet all prerequisites for casting the ritual to choose it. At 8th level and every 4 levels thereafter, you learn another uncommon ritual with a level no higher than half your level and for which you meet the prerequisites. You can cast these as the primary caster, but you can't teach them to anyone else or allow someone else to serve as primary caster unless they know the ritual as well.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the ritualist archetype. If you retrain out of this feat, you lose the rituals learned from it.

<title level="2">Ritualist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Efficient Rituals](/Feats.aspx?ID=2044), [Flexible Ritualist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2042), [Resourceful Ritualist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2043)
name: Ritualist Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Prerequisites**
[Efficient Rituals](/Feats.aspx?ID=2044)

</column>

---

You can perform [rituals](/Rules.aspx?ID=777) in only a fraction of the usual time. If a ritual has a casting time measured in days, you can cast it in an equal number of hours. If this results in a ritual longer than 8 hours, you split the ritual between multiple days, as normal for a ritual.
name: Speedy Rituals

</column>

---

You are a highly skilled scout, capable of providing your allies a timely warning of any danger. You gain the [Scout's Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=505) ranger feat. When you're using the [Scout](/Actions.aspx?ID=518) exploration activity, you grant your allies a +2 circumstance bonus to their initiative rolls instead of a +1 circumstance bonus.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the scout archetype.

[Scroll Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2053) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 189

**Archetype** [Scroll Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=74)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You've studied [scrolls](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=52) in depth. This might have been a comprehensive education in formal setting, or the sort of education where you somehow obtain a number of scrolls and try not to explode anything you didn't mean to explode. You gain the [Trick Magic Item](/Feats.aspx?ID=857) feat, and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to skill checks to Trick scrolls. If you roll a critical failure to Trick a Magic Item that's a scroll, you get a failure instead.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the scroll trickster archetype.

<title level="2">Scroll Trickster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Scroll Cache](/Feats.aspx?ID=2054), [Skim Scroll](/Feats.aspx?ID=2055)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Basic Scroll Cache](/Feats.aspx?ID=2054) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 189

**Archetype** [Scroll Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=74)

**Prerequisites**
[Scroll Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2053)

</column>

---

You have a vast and overflowing collection of scroll scraps, riddled with errors and misspellings and leaking energy like a sieve. With enough care, you can coax these scroll scraps into functioning—briefly. Each day during your daily preparations, you can create a single temporary scroll containing a 1st-level spell. The spell must be a common spell from the _Core Rulebook_, or another spell you learned via [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25), and it must come from a tradition in which you have the corresponding skill trained. This scroll is an unstable, temporary item and loses its magic the next time you make your daily preparations if you haven't already used it. It can't be used to Learn the Spell. 

At 8th level, add a second temporary scroll containing a 2nd-level spell.

<title level="2">Basic Scroll Cache leads to...</title>
[Expert Scroll Cache](/Feats.aspx?ID=2056)
name: Basic Scroll Cache

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scrounger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2058) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 190

**Archetype** [Scrounger](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=75)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

You can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) items even without appropriate tools or a workshop, though you take a –2 item penalty to your [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check. Additionally, you don't need a physical [formula book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=22) to remember all of your formulas; you pay the same cost as normal to learn them, but you memorize them all.

 You can Craft temporary items out of anything, anywhere, with whatever materials happen to be on hand, spending only 10 minutes to perform the initial Crafting check. The temporary item must be common, non-magical, 1st level or lower, and must be a weapon, armor, or a nonconsumable piece of [adventuring gear](/Equipment.aspx?Category=1). Instead of a single item, you can create 10 pieces of a single type of ammunition. This is a [shoddy item](/Rules.aspx?ID=197), but you don't take the normal penalty when using shoddy items you made using this feat. Your temporary item lasts for 1d4 hours before falling apart into its raw components; the GM rolls the number of hours secretly. You can create only the physical item, not any information or magic, so for example, while you could create a blank journal or one of random pages, you couldn't use it as a [scholarly journal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=64) or a [religious text](/Equipment.aspx?ID=42).

 You can incorporate any materials or items that you have on hand, even if they're not the type of materials that would ordinarily be used to Craft a given item, though you must have enough volume of material to make the item you want. Unless all the materials you used were an appropriate type to make the item, you take a –5 penalty to the Crafting check (or a –10 penalty if the materials you used were particularly unsuitable, as determined by the GM).

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the scrounger archetype.

<title level="2">Scrounger Dedication leads to...</title>
[Expert Disassembler](/Feats.aspx?ID=2061), [High-Quality Scrounger](/Feats.aspx?ID=2060), [Reverse Engineering](/Feats.aspx?ID=2059)
name: Scrounger Dedication
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 190

Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</additional-info>

</summary>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reverse Engineering](/Feats.aspx?ID=2059) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); [Scrounger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2058)

</column>

---

You are able to reverse engineer items into formulas more effectively than most. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks to reverse engineer a formula from an item. Additionally, if you get a critical success on your Crafting check, you can opt to not only create the formula but also reassemble the original item at the same time, leaving you with the formula and the item instead of the formula and raw materials equal to half the item's value.
name: Reverse Engineering

You can instead Craft it for someone else's use, allowing them to avoid taking the penalty for using a shoddy item when using it, but causing you to take the penalty if you use it.

    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sentinel Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2062) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 191

**Archetype** [Sentinel](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=76)

</column>

---

You have trained carefully to maximize the protective qualities of your armor. You become trained in light armor and medium armor. If you already were trained in light armor and medium armor, you gain training in heavy armor as well. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in any type of armor (but not unarmored defense), you also gain that proficiency in the armor types granted to you by this feat. If you are at least 13th level and you have a class feature that grants you expert proficiency in unarmored defense, you also become an expert in the armor types granted to you by this feat.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the sentinel archetype.

<title level="2">Sentinel Dedication leads to...</title>
[Armor Specialist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2064), [Armored Rebuff](/Feats.aspx?ID=2065), [Mighty Bulwark](/Feats.aspx?ID=2066), [Steel Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=2063)
name: Sentinel Dedication

    pfs="Standard"

[Armored Rebuff](/Feats.aspx?ID=2065) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

[Sentinel Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2062)

**Trigger**
An adjacent foe critically fails an attack roll to Strike you with a melee weapon or unarmed attack.

**Requirements**
You are wearing medium armor or heavier.

</column>

---

You rebuff puny attacks with your armor, knocking back your foe. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) the triggering foe, even if you don't have a hand free. If you succeed, you can't Stride to follow the foe, as you're knocking the foe back with the rebounded attack, not by physically moving towards them.
name: Armored Rebuff

Master in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15); Expert in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

You dedicate yourself to the shadows. Your coloring becomes gray and gloomy, your senses adjust to the darkness, and you can sneak through darkness with ease. You gain [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12). You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks when you are within dim light or darkness.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the shadowdancer archetype.

<title level="2">Shadowdancer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Shadow Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=2069), [Shadow Master](/Feats.aspx?ID=2073), [Shadow Sneak Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=2068)

</column>

---

You gain the ability to fuse and play with shadows by using focus spells specific to shadowdancers. You learn a shadowdancer focus spell of your choice: [_dance of darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=662) or [_shadow jump_](/Spells.aspx?ID=664). If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by meditating within an area of dim light or darkness. The rules for focus spells appear [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=276).

Shadowdancer focus spells are occult spells. You become trained in occult spell attack rolls and spell DCs, and your spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

<title level="2">Shadow Magic leads to...</title>
[Additional Shadow Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=2070), [Shadow Illusion](/Feats.aspx?ID=2071), [Shadow Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=2072)

When you attempt a Reflex saving throw in an area of dim light or darkness, you can roll twice and take the higher result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Snarecrafter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2074) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 194

**Archetype** [Snarecrafter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=78)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); [Snare Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=843)

</column>

---

You've learned to create a limited number of snares at no cost. Each day during your daily preparations, you can prepare four [snares](/Equipment.aspx?Category=31) from your [formula book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=22) for quick deployment; if they normally take 1 minute to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43), you can Craft them with 3 Interact actions. The number of snares increases to six if you're a master in Crafting and eight if you're legendary. Snares prepared in this way don't cost you any resources to Craft.

**Special** The feat counts as [Snare Specialist](/Feats.aspx?ID=506) for the purpose of feat prerequisites. If you also have the Snare Specialist ranger feat, the quick snares from both feats are cumulative. You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the snarecrafter archetype.

<title level="2">Snarecrafter Dedication leads to...</title>
[Giant Snare](/Feats.aspx?ID=2077), [Plentiful Snares](/Feats.aspx?ID=2078), [Remote Trigger](/Feats.aspx?ID=2076), [Surprise Snare](/Feats.aspx?ID=2075), [Trapsmith Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3134), [Trapsmith Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8636)
name: Snarecrafter Dedication

The snare automatically triggers, but it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to any applicable save DC, as well as any attack rolls or other checks the snare attempts.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Giant Snare](/Feats.aspx?ID=2077) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 194

**Archetype** [Snarecrafter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=78)

**Prerequisites**
[Snarecrafter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2074)

</column>

---

You can create bigger snares for quick deployment, making it more likely for a creature to walk into your snares. When you prepare snares for quick deployment, you can prepare some of them as giant snares. A giant snare takes up a 10-foot-by-10-foot area but costs two of your quick deployment snares. A giant snare can trigger from a creature entering any portion of its area, and all its effects apply over the full area.
name: Giant Snare

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talisman Dabbler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2079) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 195

**Archetype** [Talisman Dabbler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=79)

</column>

---

You are trained in the use of [talismans](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=19) and similar magical paraphernalia. This training might have occurred in a formal classroom or been an agglomeration of folk magic picked up over time. You can craft talismans and know the formulas for all common talismans in the _Core Rulebook_ of your level or lower. You remember talisman formulas and don't need a [formula book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=22) for them.

 Additionally, you carry a collection of magical baubles you can turn into temporary talismans. Each day during your daily preparations, you can make two talismans with an item level no higher than half your level. You must know each talisman's formula. A talisman created this way is a temporary item and loses its magic the next time you make your daily preparations if you haven't already used it.

 Finally, when you [Affix a Talisman](/Actions.aspx?ID=520), you can (in any combination) affix or remove up to four talismans in the 10-minute span.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the talisman dabbler archetype.

<title level="2">Talisman Dabbler Dedication leads to...</title>
[Deeper Dabbler](/Feats.aspx?ID=2081), [Quick Fix](/Feats.aspx?ID=2080), [Talismanic Sage](/Feats.aspx?ID=2082)
name: Talisman Dabbler Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quick Fix](/Feats.aspx?ID=2080) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 195

**Archetype** [Talisman Dabbler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=79)

**Prerequisites**
[Talisman Dabbler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2079)

</column>

---

You can attach a talisman using only a bit of glue and some string. You gain the [Rapid Affixture](/Feats.aspx?ID=2145) skill feat, even if you don't meet the prerequisites. When you use it, you can affix or remove up to four talismans in 1 minute instead of just one. 

You gain the ability to [Affix a Talisman](/Actions.aspx?ID=520) as a 3-action activity from that feat at 12th level, regardless of your [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) proficiency.
name: Quick Fix

**Special** You can select this feat a second time if you are 14th level or higher.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talismanic Sage](/Feats.aspx?ID=2082) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 195

**Archetype** [Talisman Dabbler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=79)

**Prerequisites**
[Talisman Dabbler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2079)

</column>

---

You have forgotten more about talismans than lesser warriors have ever known. Normally, affixing more than one talisman to an item causes the talismans to be suppressed, but when you [Affix a Talisman](/Actions.aspx?ID=520), you can specially treat one item you're working on, allowing it to have two active talismans at once. This special treatment ends if you use Affix a Talisman to treat a new item for this ability.
name: Talismanic Sage

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 196

**Archetypes** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80), [Gray Gardener](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=124) (Level 2)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

You have two identities, each with its own name, alignment, and abilities. Your social identity is an upstanding member of society, while your vigilante identity is a skilled and cunning warrior. Neither of these identities is a false front; you really are both of these people, and as such, your two identities can be at most one alignment step from each other (for instance, you could be neutral in one identity and neutral evil in the other, but not chaotic evil in the other). If someone attempts to discern your other identity, they must use a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to attempt a Perception check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC, as if you were using the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) action. Your Deception DC against such attempts is 20 + your proficiency modifier instead of the normal DC. Unlike with Impersonate, you don't have to attempt a Deception check to interact with someone to conceal your other identity—a check happens only if someone else specifically tries to uncover your other identity.

 Changing from one identity to the other takes 1 minute and must be done out of sight from other creatures. As this process involves both physical changes such as clothing and makeup along with an altered state of mind, other effects that change your appearance don't reduce the time required to change identities.

 Your two identities are completely distinct. You have your current identity's alignment for effects that rely on alignment, though you are eligible for abilities that require a certain alignment (such as a cleric's deity) only if both of your identities qualify. Checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about one of your identities don't reveal information about the other unless the person attempting the check knows both identities are the same person. Effects that detect you based on your identity, such as the [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) spell, work only if you are currently in the identity the effect is trying to detect; otherwise, the effect fails as if the target didn't exist.

 To maintain the separation between your identities, some of your abilities have one of two traits: [social](/Traits.aspx?ID=325) or [vigilante](/Traits.aspx?ID=326). You can use social feats only while in your social identity. Your class feats and vigilante feats are associated with your vigilante identity, and using them while in your social identity risks exposing you as a vigilante. This means that if you were particularly meek or inconspicuous before you took this feat, your identity could be at greater risk of exposure. If your identity is exposed to the public, you lose the benefits of Vigilante Dedication to disguising yourself, but you can otherwise use both social and vigilante abilities in either identity unless they rely on your identity being a secret. You can use feats that don't have either trait regardless of your current identity.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the vigilante archetype.

<title level="2">Vigilante Dedication leads to...</title>
[Accurate Swing](/Feats.aspx?ID=3334), [Discerning Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=3332), [Execution](/Feats.aspx?ID=3338), [Executioner Weapon Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=3329), [Frightful Condemnation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3333), [Gardener's Resolve](/Feats.aspx?ID=3335), [Harsh Judgment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330), [Hidden Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=2084), [It Was Me All Along!](/Feats.aspx?ID=4152), [Many Guises](/Feats.aspx?ID=2091), [Minion Guise](/Feats.aspx?ID=2085), [Quick Change](/Feats.aspx?ID=2089), [Reminder of the Greater Fear](/Feats.aspx?ID=3336), [Safe House](/Feats.aspx?ID=2086), [Social Purview](/Feats.aspx?ID=2087), [Startling Appearance (Vigilante)](/Feats.aspx?ID=2088), [Strident Command](/Feats.aspx?ID=3337), [Subjective Truth](/Feats.aspx?ID=2090), [Terrifying Countenance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3339), [Voice of Authority](/Feats.aspx?ID=3331)
name: Vigilante Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hidden Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=2084) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 196

**Archetype** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

</column>

---

You've learned to hide the magical auras of your gear. During your daily preparations, you carefully tweak any or all of your magic items to appear non-magical. Objects adjusted in this way remain so until your next preparations. A spellcaster using [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) or [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) must succeed at a Perception check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC to see through your obfuscations.
name: Hidden Magic
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)
